title: 3 Establishing Policies for Current Account Services Online
body_text:
3	Establishing Policies for Current Account Services Online
3.1	Policy on Restricting Access to CAS Online
CAS Online gives users access to Central 1 account balances and transaction history in near real time. Financial institutions are responsible for designating their own CAS Online users. Practice a high level of due diligence when appointing CAS Online users.
3.2	Policy on Internal CAS Online Controls and User Management
Establish security practices and internal controls for monitoring CAS Online activity. It is recommended that internal controls and procedures relating to the use of CAS Online are established to prevent or detect errors in a timely manner. Operations staff and external and internal auditors should pay particular attention to internal controls for CAS Online access.
Policies may include but arenГўВҖВҷt limited to the following:
Ensuring that CAS Online users have their own unique user IDs and donГўВҖВҷt share user IDs
Deleting CAS Online users who
ГўВҲВ’
are inactive for one year or more,
ГўВҲВ’
no longer require CAS Online access, or
ГўВҲВ’
are no longer employees
Ensuring that CAS Online users are reviewed on a regular basis and that action, if required, is taken to update or delete CAS Online profiles
3.3	Policy on Passwords
Secure passwords for CAS Online users are necessary to prevent unauthorized access to the system. A password breach could result in any of the following:
Theft of data or confidential information
Destruction of network systems
Deletion of data
Employee impersonation
Fraud
Breach of member confidentiality
Damage to the reputation of the financial institution if the damage done by the password breach is significant
To prevent these risks, it is recommended that financial institutions establish policies regarding the secure use of passwords. Financial institutions can include the following guidelines in their policies:
One-time passwords must be
ГўВҲВ’
unique for each CAS Online user,
ГўВҲВ’
communicated in a secure and confidential manner, and
ГўВҲВ’
changed by the CAS Online user as quickly as possible.
Characteristics of secure passwords:
ГўВҲВ’
Must be a minimum of eight characters in length
ГўВҲВ’
Can contain alphanumeric and special characters, including capital letters
ГўВҲВ’
CanГўВҖВҷt be the same password used as one of the last six passwords
Passwords must
not
contain common acronyms, including financial institution name abbreviations, and words or numbers that have personal significance to the end user, such as birth dates or the names of relatives and pets.
Passwords should be specific to the organization and shouldnГўВҖВҷt be used as passwords on websites or any other computer systems. It is recommended to use a pass-phrase as these are harder to guess (e.g., ГўВҖВңdeskchairphonemugГўВҖВқ or ГўВҖВңI LOVE ice Cream dayГўВҖВқ).
Passwords are checked against a list of known insecure passwords to prevent use of passwords that are known to have been exposed or shared from internet data breaches. This means that some password changes will fail even though the new password meets the structure requirements. If this occurs, the user should select a new password or use a pass-phrase instead.
Rules for Regular Password Updates
CAS Online users must change their passwords every six months. CAS Online users should avoid reusing previous passwords. The last six passwords canГўВҖВҷt be reused.
Rules for Secure Password Use
To prevent a password breach, it is recommended that financial institutions establish rules regarding password use:
Passwords should be memorized and not written down.
Users shouldnГўВҖВҷt give their password to a co-worker for any reason. This includes network operations and Help Desk staff.
Where possible, users should have their own login ID and password. Shared login IDs and passwords should be avoided.
Care should be taken when entering a password. Users should occasionally check to see if co-workers can see them as they enter their password into a computer.
Reporting of Password Breaches
If CAS Online users think that their password has been compromised, they must
report the incident to their immediate supervisor, and
change the password immediately.
CAS Online users must be made aware that they are responsible for their passwords, and that they will be held partly responsible for any operational misuse that occurs under their account.
END
